workflow_rationale: Adding two new provider implementations (CodexProvider, GeminiProvider) following the established Provider trait pattern from spec 030. Each provider has multiple data source fallbacks, requiring ordered implementation of OAuth → RPC/API → PTY approaches.

phases.name: Codex Provider
phases.description: Build CodexProvider with three data source fallbacks: OAuth API, JSON-RPC, and PTY screen parsing. Each subtask adds one data source layer to the fetch() method.

subtasks.description: Scaffold CodexProvider with detect() and OAuth API fetch path. Create codex.rs with: (1) CodexCredentials struct for ~/.codex/auth.json (access_token, refresh_token, last_refresh), (2) binary_on_path() helper using std::process::Command::new("which"), (3) detect() checking auth.json OR codex on PATH, (4) OAuth fetch: read creds, check refresh (>8 days = warn), GET https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/wham/usage with Bearer token, (5) parse_usage_response() helper mapping session.percent_used → primary_pct and weekly.percent_used → secondary_pct, (6) unit tests for parse helper with mock JSON.

verification.command: cd /Users/studio/bop && cargo test -p bop -- providers::codex --no-fail-fast 2>&1 | tail -20

subtasks.notes: CodexProvider scaffolded with: CodexCredentials struct, binary_on_path() helper, detect() checking auth.json OR codex on PATH, OAuth fetch with Bearer token to chatgpt.com/backend-api/wham/usage, parse_usage_response() mapping session.percent_used→primary_pct and weekly.percent_used→secondary_pct, check_refresh_staleness() warning if >8 days old, 14 unit tests all passing. Clippy clean, fmt clean.

subtasks.description: Add JSON-RPC fallback to CodexProvider fetch(). When OAuth creds are missing or expired, spawn `codex -s read-only -a untrusted app-server` via tokio::process::Command with piped stdin/stdout. Send initialize request (id:1), read response, send account/rateLimits/read (id:2), read response, parse windows array for session+weekly percent_used, kill process. 5-second total timeout. Add unit tests for RPC response parsing.

verification.command: cd /Users/studio/bop && cargo test -p bop -- providers::codex --no-fail-fast 2>&1 | tail -20

subtasks.notes: JSON-RPC fallback added to CodexProvider fetch(). When OAuth creds are missing or token rejected (401/403), spawns `codex -s read-only -a untrusted app-server` via tokio::process::Command with piped stdin/stdout. Sends initialize (id:1), reads response, sends account/rateLimits/read (id:2), reads response, parses windows array for session+weekly percent_used, kills process. 5-second total timeout. Added RPC response types (RpcResponse, RpcRateLimitWindow, RpcRateLimitsResult), parse_rpc_response() helper, fetch_via_rpc() with timeout, rpc_exchange() for the actual protocol. 9 new unit tests: valid windows, empty windows, session-only, clamping, malformed JSON, RPC error, missing result, snake_case aliases, credits field. All 23 tests pass. Clippy clean, fmt clean.

subtasks.description: Add PTY fallback to CodexProvider fetch(). When JSON-RPC also fails, spawn `codex` via tokio::process::Command, write `/status\n` to stdin, wait up to 3 seconds, read stdout, parse rendered screen for '5h limit: NN%' and 'Weekly limit: NN%' using regex or string search. Add parse test for PTY output.

verification.command: cd /Users/studio/bop && cargo test -p bop -- providers::codex --no-fail-fast 2>&1 | tail -20

subtasks.notes: PTY fallback added to CodexProvider fetch(). When both OAuth and JSON-RPC fail, spawns `codex` process, writes `/status\n` to stdin, waits up to 3 seconds, reads stdout, and parses for '5h limit: NN%' and 'Weekly limit: NN%' patterns using string search (no regex dep needed). Added: parse_pty_output(), extract_pct_after(), fetch_via_pty(), pty_exchange(), fetch_via_rpc_or_pty() chaining method. fetch() now chains: OAuth → RPC → PTY. 10 new tests: valid output, session-only, weekly-only, no percentages, empty output, clamping, ANSI noise, extract_pct_after basic/missing/no-digits. All 33 codex tests pass. Clippy clean, fmt clean.

phases.name: Gemini Provider
phases.description: Build GeminiProvider with OAuth quota API and PTY fallback. The OAuth path is more complex than Codex — requires extracting client ID/secret from the gemini npm package's oauth2.js, token refresh, and two Google API calls.

subtasks.description: Scaffold GeminiProvider with detect() and OAuth credential/token infrastructure. Create gemini.rs with: (1) GeminiCredentials struct for ~/.gemini/oauth_creds.json (access_token, refresh_token, expiry_date, id_token), (2) detect() checking oauth_creds.json OR gemini on PATH, (3) find_gemini_binary() — locate gemini on PATH, (4) extract_oauth_client() — find .../node_modules/@google/gemini-cli-core/dist/src/code_assist/oauth2.js relative to binary, regex-extract OAUTH_CLIENT_ID and OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET, (5) refresh_token_if_expired() — POST https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token with client_id/secret/refresh_token, (6) unit tests for credential parsing and client ID extraction from sample JS.

verification.command: cd /Users/studio/bop && cargo test -p bop -- providers::gemini --no-fail-fast 2>&1 | tail -20

subtasks.notes: GeminiProvider scaffolded with: GeminiCredentials struct for ~/.gemini/oauth_creds.json (access_token, refresh_token, expiry_date as f64, id_token), detect() checking oauth_creds.json OR gemini on PATH, find_gemini_binary() via `which` with symlink resolution, extract_oauth_client() walking up from binary to find node_modules/@google/gemini-cli-core/dist/src/code_assist/oauth2.js and extracting OAUTH_CLIENT_ID/OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET via string parsing, is_token_expired() handling both epoch-seconds and epoch-milliseconds, refresh_token_if_expired() POST to oauth2.googleapis.com/token, stub fetch() with TODO for subtask-2-2. Added `pub mod gemini;` to mod.rs. 23 unit tests all passing. Clippy clean, fmt clean.

subtasks.description: Implement Gemini OAuth quota fetch. In fetch(), after refreshing token: (1) POST loadCodeAssist to get cloudaicompanionProject, (2) POST retrieveUserQuota with project ID (or empty string if loadCodeAssist fails), (3) parse response array of {remainingFraction, resetTime, modelId}, (4) map: primary_pct = (1 - min(remainingFraction for Pro models)) * 100, secondary_pct = same for Flash models, primary_label = 'Pro', secondary_label = 'Flash', (5) unit tests for quota response parsing.

verification.command: cd /Users/studio/bop && cargo test -p bop -- providers::gemini --no-fail-fast 2>&1 | tail -20

subtasks.notes: Implemented Gemini OAuth quota fetch in fetch(): (1) loadCodeAssist POST to get cloudaicompanionProject (best-effort, falls back to empty string), (2) retrieveUserQuota POST with project ID, (3) parse_quota_response() maps remainingFraction to primary_pct (Pro models) and secondary_pct (Flash models) using min(remainingFraction) across model variants, (4) earliest resetTime tracked for reset_at. Added response types: LoadCodeAssistResponse, QuotaEntry, QuotaResponse (handles both object{quotas:[...]} and array[...] shapes). 15 new unit tests: valid pro+flash, pro-only, flash-only, empty entries, no remaining fraction, unknown model, clamping, case-insensitive, missing model_id, full usage, no usage, object shape deserialization, array shape deserialization, loadCodeAssist response, missing project. All 38 gemini tests pass. Clippy clean, fmt clean.

subtasks.description: Add PTY fallback to GeminiProvider fetch(). When OAuth fails, spawn `gemini` via tokio::process::Command, write `/stats model\n` to stdin, wait up to 3 seconds, read stdout, parse output for request counts and limits. Add parse test for PTY output.

verification.command: cd /Users/studio/bop && cargo test -p bop -- providers::gemini --no-fail-fast 2>&1 | tail -20

subtasks.notes: PTY fallback added to GeminiProvider fetch(). When OAuth fails (missing creds, missing client ID/secret, token refresh failure, or quota API error), spawns `gemini` process, writes `/stats model\n` to stdin, waits up to 3 seconds, reads stdout, and parses for model usage data. Parser handles fraction patterns (N/M → percentage), direct percentages (NN%), and model classification via case-insensitive "pro"/"flash" detection on each line. Added: extract_fraction_pct(), extract_direct_pct(), extract_line_pct(), parse_pty_output(), fetch_via_pty(), pty_exchange() methods. 22 new tests (12 PTY parse scenarios + 10 helper function tests). All 60 gemini tests pass. Clippy clean, fmt clean.

phases.name: Integration & Verification
phases.description: Wire CodexProvider and GeminiProvider into the provider registry, run full test suite, clippy, and fmt to verify acceptance criteria.

subtasks.description: Register both providers in mod.rs. Add `pub mod codex;` and `pub mod gemini;` to the module declarations at the top of providers/mod.rs. Update all_providers() to include CodexProvider and GeminiProvider: vec![Box::new(claude::ClaudeProvider::new()), Box::new(codex::CodexProvider::new()), Box::new(gemini::GeminiProvider::new())]. Run full acceptance criteria: cargo build -p bop, cargo test -p bop (all provider tests), cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo fmt -- --check. Verify `bop providers --json` output shape.

verification.command: cd /Users/studio/bop && cargo build -p bop 2>&1 | tail -5 && cargo test -p bop -- providers:: --no-fail-fast 2>&1 | tail -20 && cargo clippy -p bop -- -D warnings 2>&1 | tail -5 && cargo fmt -p bop -- --check 2>&1 | tail -5
verification.expected: All commands succeed with no errors or warnings

subtasks.notes: Updated all_providers() to include CodexProvider and GeminiProvider. All 126 provider tests pass, clippy clean (-D warnings), fmt check passes. Committed as c024393.

parallel_groups.reason: Both are independent provider implementations with no shared files. Codex creates codex.rs, Gemini creates gemini.rs. No file conflicts.

parallelism.speedup_estimate: Phases 1 and 2 could run in parallel (2x speedup for those phases), but both are single-service Rust modules. Recommend 1 worker for simplicity since phase 3 integration depends on both.

summary.startup_command: No services to start — this is a Rust library implementation. Build and test with: cargo build -p bop && cargo test -p bop -- providers::

acceptance_criteria: All existing provider tests still pass
bop providers --json includes codex and gemini entries (when credentials exist)

verification_steps.name: Full test suite
verification_steps.expected_outcome: All tests pass including existing provider tests

verification_strategy.reasoning: Medium risk: adding two new provider implementations following an established trait pattern. No existing code is modified except adding module declarations and registration. Unit tests cover all parsing logic. Network-dependent code uses the established error-snapshot pattern.
